reasoning: Paul Biedermann set the men's 200m freestyle long course world record at 1:42.00 in 2009 during the 'supersuit' era. This record has proven to be extraordinarily resilient, with the closest active swimmer, David Popovici, achieving a time of 1:42.97 in 2022 — nearly a full second slower. Moreover, the long course World Aquatics Championships were just held in July 2025, and major international long course competitions generally do not take place between November and January (which is typically short course season or off-season for peak peaking). Therefore, the likelihood of anyone breaking 1:42.00 in late 2025 or early 2026 is exceptionally low. Additionally, the 10-year statute of limitations for retrospective doping bans by WADA has long since elapsed, largely ruling out the chance of the record being annulled. Taking into account the system constraints on maximum probability values and the required increase in uncertainty over longer horizons, my probabilities remain as high as permitted.
